---
title: "Featured Work"
---

## Featured Work
### Teaching Students How to Better Manage Natural Resources (2025)
**Featured by the University of Texas at Arlington**
UTA highlighted work connected to our NSF-funded project supporting undergraduate learning about water resources management, decision-making, and game theory through the redesign of **ENVR 2316: Conservation of Natural Resources**.
The project explores how students learn to reason about shared natural resources, stakeholder perspectives, and evidence-based environmental decision-making.
[Read the UTA feature](https://www.uta.edu/news/news-releases/2025/10/13/teaching-students-how-to-better-manage-natural-resources)

### HSRU Postdoctoral Fellowship Cohort Feature (2025–2026)
**Featured by the Alliance of Hispanic-Serving Research Universities (HSRU)**
Selected as a fellow in the inaugural **HSRU Postdoctoral Fellowship Cohort Program**, a competitive multi-institutional initiative supporting preparation for faculty careers at research-intensive Hispanic-Serving Institutions (HSIs).
The fellowship highlights my work in discipline-based education research, water and environmental literacy, systems thinking, and evidence-based decision-making in environmental education.
[View HSRU Fellow Profile](https://www.hsru.org/initiatives/postdoc-program/fellows)

### Journal of Geoscience Education — Article of the Month (September 2024)
**Selected by the National Association of Geoscience Teachers (NAGT)**
Our article,
*The Food–Energy–Water Nexus: Assessing Undergraduate Students’ Decision Making About Complex Socio-Hydrologic Issues Supported by Hydroviz*  
(**Mostacedo-Marasovic, White, & Forbes, 2024**),
was recognized as the **Journal of Geoscience Education (JGE) Article of the Month** by NAGT in September 2024.
The work examines undergraduate students’ systems thinking and decision-making about socio-hydrologic issues using Hydroviz and Food–Energy–Water Nexus learning contexts.
[View publication (DOI)](https://doi.org/10.1080/10899995.2024.2358726)
